vulnerability.title: Configure Notification of Post-AIDE Scan Details
vulnerability.description: AIDE should notify appropriate personnel of the details of a scan after the scan has been run. If AIDE has already been configured for periodic execution in /etc/crontab, append the following line to the existing AIDE line: | /bin/mail -s "$(hostname) - AIDE Integrity Check" root@localhost Otherwise, add the following line to /etc/crontab: 05 4 * * * root /usr/sbin/aide --check | /bin/mail -s "$(hostname) - AIDE Integrity Check" root@localhost AIDE can be executed periodically through other means; this is merely one example.
vulnerability.recommendation: Remediation script: # Remediation is applicable only in certain platforms if rpm --quiet -q kernel-core; then if ! rpm -q --quiet "aide" ; then dnf install -y "aide" fi var_aide_scan_notification_email='' CRONTAB=/etc/crontab CRONDIRS='/etc/cron.d /etc/cron.daily /etc/cron.weekly /etc/cron.monthly' # NOTE: on some platforms, /etc/crontab may not exist if [ -f /etc/crontab ]; then CRONTAB_EXIST=/etc/crontab fi if [ -f /var/spool/cron/root ]; then VARSPOOL=/var/spool/cron/root fi if ! grep -qR '^.*/usr/sbin/aide\s*\-\-check.*|.*\/bin\/mail\s*-s\s*".*"\s*.*@.*$' $CRONTAB_EXIST $VARSPOOL $CRONDIRS; then echo "0 5 * * * root /usr/sbin/aide --check | /bin/mail -s \"\$(hostname) - AIDE Integrity Check\" $var_aide_scan_notification_email" >> $CRONTAB fi else >&2 echo 'Remediation is not applicable, nothing was done'

triage_decision.reason: The remediation only appends a cron entry to run AIDE and pipe its output to the local mail command. This does not modify authentication, networking, or critical boot components, and the change is reversible. The risk of locking out access is minimal.
triage_decision.estimated_impact: filesystems affected; Incorrect mail address or empty variable may result in undelivered notifications; Cron syntax error could cause the cron job to be ignored; Mail utility may not be installed or configured

execution_details.stdout:
SHELL=/bin/bash
PATH=/sbin:/bin:/usr/sbin:/usr/bin
MAILTO=root

# For details see man 4 crontabs

# Example of job definition:
# .---------------- minute (0 - 59)
# |  .------------- hour (0 - 23)
# |  |  .---------- day of month (1 - 31)
# |  |  |  .------- month (1 - 12) OR jan,feb,mar,apr ...
# |  |  |  |  .---- day of week (0 - 6) (Sunday=0 or 7) OR sun,mon,tue,wed,thu,fri,sat
# |  |  |  |  |
# *  *  *  *  * user-name  command to be executed

previous_attempts.scan_output: Vulnerability openscap_003 not found in single-rule scan (possibly fixed)

llm_verdict.message: I’m glad the remediation worked! If you have any other OpenSCAP findings, configuration tweaks, or security‑hardening tasks you’d like to tackle, just let me know the details (rule name/ID, the file or service involved, and any specific requirements). I’ll walk through the inspection, apply the appropriate changes, and verify that everything passes. Feel free to share the next item you’d like addressed!

review_feedback: The remediation adds a cron entry but leaves the recipient address empty, resulting in no notification being sent. It also lacks idempotency, does not backup the original crontab, and includes unnecessary checks. As written, it does not satisfy the requirement to mail AIDE scan details. Suggestions: Set var_aide_scan_notification_email to a valid address such as root@localhost or make it configurable by the administrator.; Add logic to detect an existing AIDE cron line and modify it in place rather than always appending, ensuring idempotency.; Create a backup of /etc/crontab (e.g., /etc/crontab.bak) before editing.; Remove the irrelevant kernel-core check.; Install and verify the presence of a mail client (mailx/postfix) before adding the pipe.; Use a more precise grep or awk to search only /etc/crontab and the root crontab file, avoiding -R on whole directories.; Consider using sed to edit the existing line preserving any custom options.

previous_review_verdicts.feedback: The remediation adds a cron entry but leaves the recipient address empty, resulting in no notification being sent. It also lacks idempotency, does not backup the original crontab, and includes unnecessary checks. As written, it does not satisfy the requirement to mail AIDE scan details.
concerns: Variable var_aide_scan_notification_email is set to an empty string, so the mail command has no recipient.
The grep check uses a pattern that expects an email address (.+@.+); because the address is empty the check will never match, leading to duplicate cron lines on repeated runs.
No backup of /etc/crontab is created before modification.
Unnecessary platform check for kernel-core unrelated to AIDE.
Does not verify that the /bin/mail (mailx) command is installed.
Appends to /etc/crontab without confirming file existence or proper permissions.
Potentially runs grep -R on large directories, which is inefficient.
suggested_improvements: Set var_aide_scan_notification_email to a valid address such as root@localhost or make it configurable by the administrator.
Add logic to detect an existing AIDE cron line and modify it in place rather than always appending, ensuring idempotency.
Create a backup of /etc/crontab (e.g., /etc/crontab.bak) before editing.
Install and verify the presence of a mail client (mailx/postfix) before adding the pipe.
Use a more precise grep or awk to search only /etc/crontab and the root crontab file, avoiding -R on whole directories.
Consider using sed to edit the existing line preserving any custom options.
